mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and federal funds rate hikes forces aggressive monetary tightening, which suppresses aggregate demand and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both rise, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which supports near-term corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over one week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations, or the signal reverses direction during supply-constrained regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing retail sales of automobiles reflect strong consumer discretionary spending, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rising RSAFS values are followed by negative 20-day forward returns in >58% of cases, or the monthly correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1.1 million units signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and downstream consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The market produces positive 60-day returns in >60% of periods where HOUST drops below 1100.0, or the threshold effect disappears in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of triggered months, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in panel regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank intervention, which increases equity risk premiums and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL values are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in >65% of observations, or the predictive slope becomes positive during high-inflation regimes.
